entry.prompt: Express GFP from a constitutive promoter in E. coli
Make a simple mCherry reporter driven by the J23100 promoter
Constitutive expression of beta-galactosidase (LacZ) in E. coli
Express luciferase from a T7 promoter in E. coli
Constitutive sfGFP expression using the strong J23119 promoter in E. coli
Express mScarlet-I from a medium-strength constitutive promoter in E. coli
Constitutive YFP expression with the B0034 RBS in E. coli
Express CFP under a weak constitutive promoter (J23106) in E. coli
Constitutive GFP expression from the TEF1 promoter in S. cerevisiae
Express mCherry from the GAL1 promoter in S. cerevisiae (galactose-induced)
Constitutive Venus expression under the ADH1 promoter in yeast
Express EGFP from a CMV promoter in HEK293 cells
Constitutive mCherry from the EF1a promoter in HEK293 cells
Express firefly luciferase under the PGK1 promoter in HeLa cells
Constitutive GFP under the CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis
Express GUS reporter under a ubiquitin promoter in tobacco
Constitutive GFP from the Pveg promoter in Bacillus subtilis
Express deGFP from a sigma70 promoter in an E. coli TX-TL cell-free system
Simple His-tagged mRFP expression from a constitutive promoter in E. coli
Express secreted alkaline phosphatase (PhoA) from a constitutive promoter in E. coli
IPTG-inducible mCherry reporter in E. coli using LacI repression of Ptrc
aTc-inducible GFP using TetR repression of pTet in E. coli
Arabinose-inducible RFP using AraC activation of pBAD in E. coli
Rhamnose-inducible YFP using RhaS activation of pRha in E. coli
IPTG-inducible expression of a T7 RNA polymerase, which then drives sfGFP from a T7 promoter
Build a NOT gate: constitutive TetR represses GFP; aTc turns GFP ON
Arabinose induces expression of a T7 RNAP which drives GFP from a T7 promoter
Create an aTc-inducible RFP reporter with a TetR repression module
Theophylline-responsive riboswitch controlling GFP translation in E. coli
Galactose-inducible mCherry using the GAL1 promoter in S. cerevisiae
Copper-inducible GFP using the CUP1 promoter in S. cerevisiae
Doxycycline-inducible GFP using Tet-On (rtTA + TRE promoter) in HEK293 cells
Tamoxifen-inducible Cre recombinase (CreERT2) controlling mCherry expression in HEK293
Dexamethasone-inducible GFP using the GVG system in Arabidopsis
Xylose-inducible GFP using the xylA promoter and XylR in Bacillus subtilis
Mercury biosensor: MerR activated by Hg2+ drives GFP from the PmerT promoter
Zinc biosensor using ZntR activation of PzntA driving mCherry
Nitrate-responsive expression of GFP using NarL activation of PnarK
Osmotic stress sensor: OmpR activation of PompC drives sfGFP upon high osmolarity
Heat-shock responsive GFP using sigma-32 (rpoH) driven PibpA promoter
AND gate where both IPTG and arabinose are required for GFP output; use LacI repression AND AraC activation converging on a hybrid promoter
OR gate: GFP is expressed if either IPTG or aTc is present, using two parallel promoters
NOT gate using PhlF repressor: constitutive PhlF represses pPhlF driving GFP; DAPG de-represses
NAND gate implemented with two repressors acting on the same output promoter
XOR gate: GFP on only if exactly one of IPTG or arabinose is present
IMPLIES gate using LacI and TetR: output ON unless LacI ON and TetR OFF
Classic Gardner-Collins-Cantor toggle switch using LacI and TetR with cross-repression; readouts via GFP (TetR branch) and RFP (LacI branch)
Toggle switch between CI and Cro (lambda-derived) with bistable output on GFP
CRISPRi-based toggle: two sgRNAs cross-repressing each other's promoters
Yeast AND gate requiring both estradiol (Z3EV) and galactose (Gal4) to turn on mCherry
Mammalian AND gate requiring doxycycline AND abscisic acid to drive mCherry in HEK293
Mammalian toggle switch between two states using TetR and PipR with CMV-driven readouts
Time-delay circuit where IPTG triggers a cascade with two intermediate repressors before GFP turns on
Majority gate over three inducers (IPTG, aTc, arabinose): GFP on if ≥2 inducers are present
NOR gate using two tandem repressor binding sites driving GFP
Implication logic: IF IPTG THEN GFP, implemented via LacI repression of pLac driving GFP
A buffer / identity gate: IPTG turns GFP ON linearly; no repression cascade
Ratio-sensor: output GFP proportional to ratio of AraC/LacI activation
Memory circuit using integrase flip: BxB1 integrase catalyzes DNA flip creating bistable on/off GFP
Sequential gate: IPTG must precede arabinose for GFP output (temporal AND)
Elowitz repressilator: three mutually repressing repressors (LacI, TetR, CI) with GFP readout
Hasty-style dual-feedback oscillator with LuxI/AiiA feedback in E. coli driving GFP pulses
Arsenic biosensor: ArsR-derepressed GFP with constitutive mCherry normalization
Copper biosensor: CusR activation of PcusC drives GFP; CueR as orthogonal control
Quorum-sensing sender: LuxI produces AHL; receiver population detects AHL via LuxR-activated GFP
Two-population relay: population A produces C4HSL via RhlI, population B detects via RhlR to make GFP
Negative autoregulation of TetR reducing noise in downstream GFP
Positive feedback loop on LacI producing hysteresis in GFP output
3-gene transcriptional cascade: arabinose → AraC → T7 RNAP → CI → lambda promoter → GFP
pH biosensor based on CadC-activated CadA promoter driving mCherry
Benzene biosensor using XylR from Pseudomonas driving GFP via the Pu promoter
Steroid biosensor in yeast using an estradiol-responsive synthetic TF driving mCherry
Glucose biosensor in HEK293 using a ChREBP-based synthetic TF activating luciferase
NF-kB negative autoregulation via IκBα feedback to reduce steady-state variability of downstream GFP
Auxin biosensor in Arabidopsis using DR5 promoter driving GUS
Incoherent feedforward loop: input activates Y and Z, Y represses Z — pulse generator for GFP
Coherent feedforward loop where two activators must both turn on for GFP with AND-like behavior
Glucose-responsive GFP using Crp-cAMP activation of a cAMP-sensitive promoter
Hypoxia biosensor: FNR-activated PnarK drives GFP only under anaerobic conditions
Mutual activation loop between two TFs producing bistability with RFP/GFP dual reporters
Violacein biosynthesis: VioA, VioB, VioC, VioD, VioE enzymes expressed as an operon from a T7 promoter
Beta-carotene production pathway: CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, CrtY expressed from pBAD arabinose-inducible operon
Reconstitute the mevalonate pathway (HMGR, ERG12, ERG8, ERG19, IDI1) in S. cerevisiae for terpenoid production
CRISPRi inverter: constitutive dCas9 with sgRNA targeting a pTarget drives GFP OFF when sgRNA is induced
CRISPRa activator using dCas9-VP64 with sgRNA recruiting a synthetic activator to upregulate endogenous gene
Multiplex CRISPRi: three sgRNAs processed by Csy4 ribozyme target three different promoters to silence three reporters
Mammalian CRISPRa with dCas9-p300 targeting a latent locus to activate endogenous HBG1 expression
Dual-input kill switch in E. coli: without both IPTG and arabinose, CcdB toxin kills cells; both inducers maintain CcdA antitoxin
Temperature-sensitive kill switch: cells die above 37°C via heat-induced MazF toxin expression
Bidirectional quorum sensing: population A (LuxI sender) and population B (LasI sender) mutually activate reciprocal reporters
Engineer a tumor-targeting E. coli Nissle circuit: hypoxia-responsive FNR promoter drives InvA; constitutive anti-HER2 nanobody surface-displayed via INP anchor
CAR-T cell with tandem CD19/CD22 CAR driven by an EF1α promoter plus a suicide switch (HSV-TK) induced by ganciclovir
Nitrogen-fixing circuit: NifH, NifD, NifK nitrogenase subunits driven by NifA-activated nifH promoter; GlnK anti-activator provides feedback under high nitrogen
Yeast galactose utilization rewiring: GAL80 knockout with GAL4-VP64 driving constitutive GAL1/7/10 expression
Synthetic auxin-responsive circuit in plant cells: auxin triggers AUX/IAA degradation via TIR1, releasing ARF to activate DR5 driving GUS
Lycopene overproduction: DXS, IDI, IspA, CrtE, CrtB, CrtI in a single operon with strong RBS tuning per enzyme
CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA sensor: Cas13 cleaves a quenched GFP mRNA when trigger RNA is present
Population-level consensus circuit using AHL-mediated quorum sensing to synchronize GFP pulses across cells
Bacillus subtilis containment circuit: constitutive expression of MazF toxin unless antitoxin MazE is expressed from a xylose-inducible promoter
Cell-free CRISPR sensor: Cas12a activates with trigger DNA, cleaving a quenched fluorescent reporter in a TX-TL reaction
